name: voicetypr
description: Voicetypr - AI powered offline voice to text dictation tool for busy founders, vibe coders, AI power users on macos, windows. Alternative to wispr flow and superwhisper.
readme_summary: Voicetypr is an open-source, offline AI-powered voice-to-text dictation tool for macOS and Windows. It offers local transcription for privacy, supports numerous languages, and can optionally enhance transcriptions using cloud AI services.
key_features: Automatic text insertion at cursor position
Multiple AI model sizes for accuracy/speed tradeoffs
Hardware acceleration (Metal on macOS, GPU on Windows)
primary_use_case: Offline voice-to-text dictation for enhanced productivity on macOS and Windows.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot with a microphone headset, sitting at a clean desk. The robot is speaking, and sound waves are visibly transforming into lines of text that flow directly into an open laptop screen displaying a document. Subtle digital particles emanate from the sound waves to the text, and small, clean UI elements representing language selection and AI enhancement are visible on a secondary screen. The overall scene is bright and modern, with a focus on seamless transformation. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with soft, clean lines and a vibrant, tech-inspired color palette, emphasizing efficiency and ease of use.

name: commonforms
description: CommonForms — open models to auto-detect PDF form fields
readme_summary: CommonForms is a Python package that automatically converts standard PDF documents into fillable forms by detecting and marking form fields. It provides both a command-line interface (CLI) and an API for this conversion process, leveraging pre-trained models like FFDNet-S and FFDNet-L.
key_features: Automatic PDF to fillable form conversion
CLI and API for form preparation
Configurable inference parameters (device, image size, confidence)
primary_use_case: To automate the process of transforming static PDF documents into interactive, fillable forms, making data entry and document processing more efficient.
cover_image_prompt: A magical scribe with a quill pen transforming a stack of blank, flat paper documents into a vibrant, interactive digital form with glowing input fields and checkboxes. The scribe is surrounded by floating data particles, and a subtle holographic screen displays the 'CommonForms' logo and a simplified pipeline diagram. The background is a clean, modern library with soft, ambient lighting. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and a slightly whimsical, futuristic feel, emphasizing the transformation from static to dynamic.

name: fort
readme_summary: Fort Firewall is a robust and user-friendly firewall application for Windows, designed to provide granular control over network traffic. It allows users to create flexible rules based on applications, addresses, ports, and protocols, with features like speed limiting and blocklists.
key_features: Flexible network rules per application or global
primary_use_case: To provide users with advanced control over network connections on their Windows machines, enhancing security and managing bandwidth usage.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant digital gatekeeper, personified as a knight in shining armor, standing guard over a bustling city of interconnected data streams. The knight holds a shield emblazoned with the 'Fort Firewall' logo, deflecting malicious-looking red data packets while allowing safe, green data packets to pass through to the city. Small, transparent screens float around the knight, displaying simple icons representing applications and their bandwidth usage. The scene is set in a clean, futuristic digital landscape with a clear focus on the gatekeeper and the flow of data. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, contrasting colors and clear, defined edges.

name: container-diet
description: Slim down your Docker images with the power of AI. Container Diet analyzes your images and Dockerfiles to provide actionable, context-aware optimization advice.
readme_summary: Container Diet is a Go-based CLI tool that optimizes Docker images using AI. It analyzes Dockerfiles and images to provide actionable advice and automatically generate optimized versions, aiming to reduce image size and improve security.
key_features: AI-powered Docker image and Dockerfile analysis
Support for 12+ AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, etc.)
MCP server for AI agent integration (e.g., in editors like Cursor)
Tools for AI agents: analyze_dockerfile, analyze_image, get_optimization_advice, get_image_summary
primary_use_case: Optimizing Docker images for size, security, and best practices using AI-driven analysis and automated fixes.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, anthropomorphic chef meticulously trimming excess fat from a large, unwieldy loaf of bread, transforming it into a perfectly shaped, compact baguette. The chef is using glowing, futuristic tools that emit digital particles. Small screens around the scene display simplified Docker layer diagrams and optimization metrics. The background is a clean, modern kitchen with subtle code snippets integrated into the tile patterns. The overall mood is one of efficiency and precision, with a bright, clean color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing the transformation and reduction process.
